blocks.text: DTC P0118: High water temperature sensor voltage

Water temperature sensors are used for air-fuel ratio feedback control, ignition timing control, and idle control. The electrical resistance of the water temperature sensor varies with the engine cooling water temperature. When the engine coolant temperature drops, the electrical resistance increases and the PGM-FI ECU detects a higher voltage. When the engine cooling water temperature rises, the electrical resistance decreases and the PGM-FI ECU detects a lower voltage. If the water temperature sensor output voltage remains above a predetermined value for a predetermined period of time, the PGM-FI ECU determines a failure and stores the DTC.
Detection frequency, detection sequence, detection time required, detection method type, OBD status

rows.text: 24-hour (e.g. observation, operation (of a machine), care (of patient))
Detection order (of the DTC in question)
1D/C (single detection method), PGM-FI warning light: On
Normal judgment, failure judgment, not completed
Ignition switch/ ENGINE START STOP switch
DTC requesting the prohibition of detection of the relevant DTC

blocks.text: Water temperature sensor output voltage [water temperature sensor] remains above 4.92V for more than 2 seconds.

items.text: Disconnection of cord (TW line) between PGM-FI ECU and water temperature sensor
Disconnection of code (SG line) between PGM-FI ECU and water temperature sensor

blocks.text: If the vehicle is determined to be faulty, the PGM-FI warning light comes on and the temporary DTC, DTC and freeze data are stored in the PGM-FI ECU memory.

If the vehicle is diagnosed and determined to be normal after running consecutive 3D/C runs, the PGM-FI warning light will turn off. In addition, the PGM-FI warning light, temporary DTC, DTC and freeze data are cleared by using the clear command of the scan tool (including HDS) or by removing the 12V battery terminal.
